cases.passage: The XYZ Company is a leading manufacturer of electronic gadgets. It has a turnover of Rs. 500 crores and has been in operation for the past 10 years. The company's financial year ends on 31st March every year. The company's financial statements for the year ended 31st March 2023, have been prepared in accordance with the Accounting Standards prescribed by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India. The financial statements include a Balance Sheet as at 31st March 2023, and a Profit and Loss Account for the year ended on that date. Prepare the Balance Sheet and Profit and Loss Account for the year ended 31st March 2023, of the XYZ Company.

questions.q: What are the financial statements prepared by the XYZ Company?
opts: Balance Sheet and Cash Flow Statement
Balance Sheet and Profit and Loss Account
Profit and Loss Account and Statement of Changes in Equity
Cash Flow Statement and Statement of Changes in Equity
questions.explanation: The financial statements prepared by the XYZ Company include a Balance Sheet and a Profit and Loss Account.

questions.q: What is the turnover of the XYZ Company?
questions.explanation: The turnover of the XYZ Company is Rs. 500 crores.

questions.q: What is the accounting standard applicable to the financial statements of the XYZ Company?
opts: Institute of Chartered Accountants of India
questions.explanation: The financial statements of the XYZ Company are prepared in accordance with the Accounting Standards prescribed by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India.

questions.q: What is the date of the financial year-end of the XYZ Company?
questions.explanation: The financial year-end of the XYZ Company is 31st March.

questions.q: What is the duration for which the XYZ Company has been in operation?
questions.explanation: The XYZ Company has been in operation for the past 10 years.

cases.passage: The ABC Company is a trading concern with a turnover of Rs. 100 crores. The company's financial year ends on 31st December every year. The company's financial statements for the year ended 31st December 2022, have been prepared in accordance with the Accounting Standards prescribed by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India. The financial statements include a Balance Sheet as at 31st December 2022, and a Profit and Loss Account for the year ended on that date. The company's cash is Rs. 50 lakhs and the bank balance is Rs. 25 lakhs. The company's assets include a motor vehicle worth Rs. 5 lakhs and stock-in-trade worth Rs. 20 lakhs. The company's liabilities include a bank loan of Rs. 10 lakhs and a trade creditor of Rs. 5 lakhs. Prepare the Balance Sheet of the ABC Company as at 31st December 2022.

questions.q: What is the turnover of the ABC Company?
questions.explanation: The turnover of the ABC Company is Rs. 100 crores.

questions.q: What is the date of the financial year-end of the ABC Company?
questions.explanation: The financial year-end of the ABC Company is 31st December.

questions.q: What is the value of the motor vehicle of the ABC Company?
questions.explanation: The motor vehicle of the ABC Company is worth Rs. 5 lakhs.

questions.q: What is the amount of the bank loan of the ABC Company?
questions.explanation: The bank loan of the ABC Company is Rs. 10 lakhs.

questions.q: What is the amount of the trade creditor of the ABC Company?
questions.explanation: The trade creditor of the ABC Company is Rs. 5 lakhs.
